zh-hans.Enmore, Somerset, England, United Kingdo: 英国英格兰薩默塞特恩莫

nameMeanings.name: Malet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: nickname from a diminutive of Mal ‘bad’ or from a derivative of the related Old French word maleit ‘accursed’ (see Mallet 1). Compare Malette .
French: from the Old French personal name Malet, a pet form of Malo (compare Mallet 2).
French: variant of   Mallet   3.

variants.name: Mal
meanings: Slovenian: nickname for a physically small man, from mal ‘small, little’.
French (mainly Nord): nickname from mal ‘bad’ (from Latin malus).
Indian (northern states): variant of   Mall  .

variants.name: Malette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gaston, Alphonse, Francois, Jacques, Rejean, Serge.
French: variant or feminine form of Malet and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this, reflecting the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Curry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Comhraidhe, ‘descendant of Comhraidhe’, a personal name of uncertain meaning.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Corra (see Corr ).
Scottish and northern English: variant of   Currie  .

variants.name: Corr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, Aidan, Brendan.
Irish:
Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Corra ‘descendant of Corra’, a personal name from corr ‘spear, pointed object’. It is occasionally translated into English as Weir (from Irish cora ‘weir’).

variants.name: Currey
meanings: Irish: variant of   Curry  .
English: habiational name from Curry in Somerset, so named after a stream possibly meaning ‘border river’ (compare Welsh cwr ‘corner, border’ or Cornish cor ‘hedge, boundary’).
English (of Norman origin): from Old French curie ‘kitchen’, and probably an occupational name for a cook or kitchen hand.

variants.name: Corey
meanings: English: possibly from the Old Norse personal names Kori (of unexplained etymology) or Kári (from kárr ‘curl’ or ‘quarrelsome’). Alternatively, a habitational name from one or more of Curry Mallet or Curry Rivel (Somerset), West Curry or Cory in Morwenstow (Cornwall), or the river name in Coryton (Devon), all of which have early spellings with -o-.
Irish (Down; Tyrone): variant of   Curry  .
Americanized form of Arabic   Khoury   or   Khouri   ‘priest’. Compare   Cory   and   Korey  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Malet II Of Curry was born in 1080, in Enmore, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, Gilbert Malet De Curry, was 25 and his mother, Mrs. Gilbert Malet, was 30. He had at least 1 son with Mrs. Robert Malet. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Malet II Of Curry est né en 1080, à Enmore, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gilbert Malet De Curry, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Gilbert Malet, avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs. Robert Malet. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Malet II Of Curry 1080 in Enmore, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gilbert Malet De Curry 25 und seine Mutter Mrs. Gilbert Malet 30. Er hatte mit Mrs. Robert Malet mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Malet II Of Curry è nato il nel 1080 nel Enmore, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Gilbert Malet De Curry, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Gilbert Malet, aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs. Robert Malet. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Enmore, Somerset, England, United Kingdo: イギリスイギリスソメルセトエンモー
ja.lifeSummary: Robert Malet II Of Curryが1080年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトエンモーで生まれた時，父Gilbert Malet De Curryは25歳，母Mrs. Gilbert Maletは30歳であった。彼はMrs. Robert Maletとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Malet II Of Curry(이)가 1080년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 모어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert Malet De Curry(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Gilbert Malet(은)는 30세였다. 그는 Mrs. Robert Malet(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

mn.Enmore, Somerset, England, United Kingdo: Enmore, Bro an Hañv, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Malet II Of Curry nasceu em 1080, em Enmore, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Gilbert Malet De Curry, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Gilbert Malet, tinha 30 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs. Robert Malet. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Malet II Of Curry родился в 1080 г. в местности энмор, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Gilbert Malet De Curry было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Gilbert Malet, 30. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs. Robert Malet. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Malet II Of Curry nació en 1080, en Enmore, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Gilbert Malet De Curry, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mrs. Gilbert Malet, tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mrs. Robert Malet. Murió en su pueblo.
